content.answer:
Hi Ian, JH,

Providing a quick update on CNSIP revamp plan and timeline in below summary
table. We have made progress on a few of them alongside local SIP
optimization efforts, and WIP on additional ones, focusing on

1) address issues that make sellers reject CNSIP: a) provide SIP shop
visibility to sellers, b) simplify settlement logic to help seller
understand the payment clearly

2) improve our internal growth levers on SKU count, price, and marketing

We are also working with Derek’s CN SMT team and James’ CN SLS team to
explore opportunities and synergies. Please expect a completed joint
proposal from us by 30 May on following initiatives.

Summary of CNSIP revamp initiatives

Focus

#

On-going initiative

Description

Current progress and impact realized

Next-steps and ETA

Impact potential (Dec-25)

More SKUs

1

Improve SKU migration

Revive non-performing SKUs with price reduction

Improve system migration efficiency with product/ops fix

Revise SIP seller onboarding criteria

Revived non-performing SKUs for 6 CNSIP lanes in mid-Apr, recovered ~1.1M
SKUs

Post 2 weeks avg +623 ADO

Conducted resync after raising shop listing limit, pending impact monitoring

Build overall SKU gap funnel per market and deep-dive into reasons why SKU
gaps are not on CNSIP, and next-steps to close the gap [ETA 28 May]

Improve migration efficiency with system/ops fix alongside local SIP lanes
[ETA Jun]

To be added by 30 May

Cheaper price

2a

Seller settlement price reduction

Push CNSIP sellers to lower settlement price to SIP

Conducted price negotiation for 5-10% discount to top SKUs on monthly basis

MTD +1.9K ADO from pitched SKU pool

After management fee removal, initiate shop-leve (instead of current SKU
level) negotiation, similarly to local SIP practice [ETA Jun]

Explore system solution to allow dynamic settlement price based on lowest
settlement price among seller’s direct shops; 2) sca

content.question: address issues that make sellers reject CNSIP: a) provide SIP shop
improve our internal growth levers on SKU count, price, and marketing

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

For existing SIP sellers, are they in all markets that they are not in with
direct model?

   -

   Yes, we open SIP shops for all markets that they currently do not have
   Direct shops in



For new SIP sellers, the low hanging fruit for me would be GGPs who are not
in all our markets. It is unclear to me whether it should be

   -

   What the size is
   -

   We pitch them direct first
   -

   We get them on SIP first, then return shop to them later



Then we will move down the value chain in terms of type of sellers to
prioritize..

   -

   Size: we are building the funnel view for each market as part of our
   proposal next week. We are assessing, for all the CNCB GPs and their SKUs,
   how many are already in a local market (via direct or via CNSIP); for the
   remaining gap, why they are not live in CNSIP (not eligible? not pitched?
   Rejected? Migration issue?)
   -

   Direct first or SIP first:
   -

      We had initial discussion with Derek/Elaine, current preference is
      Direct first
      -

      The approach is
      -

         For the target seller pool that CN SMT team wants to pitch for
         Direct (mainly short-tail and Mall sellers), CN SMT will
pitch for Direct
         first, and then pitch for CNSIP as a backup option to seller
if the sellers
         confirms not to join Direct
         -

         For the sellers outside CN SMT target pool (mainly long-tail and
         non-Mall), we pitch to CNSIP directly
         -

         Eventual goal is we have one shop in each market, Direct or SIP,
         so the SKUs are made available to the market
         -

      We will jointly finalize the collaboration plan and impact early next
      week, to share our joint proposal by 30 May

Hi Ian, JH,

Please find attached CNSIP revamp proposal for your review. The scope split
and collaborations with CNCB SMT in this doc are our joint proposal with @Cheng
Derek <derek.cheng@shopee.com> / @Elaine Wang <elaine.wang@shopee.com> / @Xu
Wu <amy.wu@shopee.com>.

Thank you.
Cuiwei

Few things I think qutie important, but missing.

1) Team effort estimation.

For listing migration, @Yiru Zhao <yiru.zhao@shopee.com> team did some
estimation, which indicates quite substaintial HC requirement for listing
QC, unperforming listing removal and GR coordinations. Need to factor in
those. Also for RMs, please share the additional processes. Their focus for
2025 are LFF and pricing based on current plan. As SKU migration is the
major uplifting of this initiative, we have to consider the additional team
effort from local Ops, Reg Ops, CNCB RMs and CNCB SIP team.

2) For more SKU initiatives, can show me the SKU efficiency assumption on
slide 13? We have to bear in mind that large portion of these non migrated
SIP SKUs could be sellers purposely choose not to populate from MTSKUs to
MPSKUs.

3) Have we done remove duplications effect from SMT market expansion
initiative?

Hi JH,


Thanks for the comments. Please kindly see below responses inputted/aligned
among CB ops / CNCB SMT / CNSIP team. Thanks a lot!



1) Team effort estimation.


For listing migration, @Yiru Zhao team did some estimation, which indicates
quite substantial HC requirement for listing QC, unperforming listing
removal and GR coordinations. Need to factor in those. Also for RMs, please
share the additional processes. Their focus for 2025 are LFF and pricing
based on current plan. As SKU migration is the major uplifting of this
initiative, we have to consider the additional team effort from local Ops,
Reg Ops, CNCB RMs and CNCB SIP team.


Listing QC [Input from Reg Listing team]

As of April 2025, SIP has significantly higher QC agent cost per order
compared to non-SIP SKUs. Cost per order is expected to increase when SIP
migration intensifies.

QC Agent Cost per '000 orders (Apr 25)

$0.17

$0.01

$0.11

% Difference

102%

1575%

2222%


As such, we proposed the following SOP to SIP to ensure they can curate
SKUs flexibly without being constrained by QC cost or volume:

   1.

   SIP SKU quality shall not rely on agent QC, most of the qc rules are
   already being put in SIP migration system.
   2.

   By default, SIP SKUs that trigger QC (e.g. flagged by Listing QC DS
   models) shall not be reviewed nor go live. You can imagine this to be an
   additional layer of filter to the existing listing validation checks such
   as logistic channel/prohibited category restrictions. Then these manual
   checks will be put in the system.
   3.

   If there are certain SKUs triggered for QC that SIP believe have high
   potential (e.g. above a certain ADO/ADGMV threshold), they can be
   whitelisted for agent review and a c
